breakClass: DateTime arithmetic helper inside an unmaterialized query: DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays/AddMinutes (plain CLR helpers in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs, NOT registered EF functions) applied to a COLUMN expression inside a server-side LINQ-to-Entities projection or Where. EF6 used DbFunctions.AddDays (canonical -> SQL DATEADD); the port swapped in DbFunctionsCompat.* which EF Core 10 cannot translate, so the query throws 'could not be translated' -> HTTP 500. Fix = EF-native (col).Value.AddDays(n ?? 0) which DOES translate to DATEADD.
why: select new of IQueryable v, then v.OrderByDescending().Take(5) (L189) then v.ToList() (L190); no AsEnumerable boundary. CLR helper untranslatable -> 500. Legacy DbFunctions.AddDays. Port regression.
finalFix:
Replace the untranslatable CLR helper with EF-native DateTime.AddDays (which EF Core 10 translates to SQL DATEADD, mirroring legacy DbFunctions.AddDays), preserving exact null->0 / non-null->QuotValidity+1 semantics and the comparison.

File: Controllers/CustomerRelationController.cs, line 187 (method GetQuotEntry).

FROM:
    validity = (DateTime.Now <= DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(b.QuotDate, (b.QuotValidity == null) ? 0 : b.QuotValidity + 1)) ? "Active" : "Expired"

TO:
    validity = (b.QuotDate.AddDays((b.QuotValidity == null) ? 0 : (b.QuotValidity.Value + 1)) >= DateTime.Now) ? "Active" : "Expired"

Rationale: QuotDate is non-nullable DateTime so call .AddDays directly (do NOT use .Value â€” it has no Value; the hunter's .Value patch would not compile). QuotValidity is int?, so use .Value in the non-null branch (the null branch is already guarded, returning 0). EF Core 10 translates DateTime.AddDays(double) -> DATEADD(day,...) and the ternary -> CASE WHEN. `AddDays(...) >= DateTime.Now` is logically identical to legacy `DateTime.Now <= AddDays(...)`, so output (Active/Expired) is unchanged.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED port-break at Controllers/CustomerRelationController.cs:187, method GetQuotEntry.

(a) SERVER-SIDE â€” confirmed. The query is built as IQueryable: `var v = (from b in db.Quotations where(...) select new { ... validity = (DateTime.Now <= DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(b.QuotDate, (b.QuotValidity==null)?0:b.QuotValidity+1)) ? "Active":"Expired" });` (L178-188). L189 `v = v.OrderByDescending(c=>c.QuotationId).Take(5);` keeps it IQueryable. The ONLY materialization is L190 `var data = v.ToList();`. There is NO `.ToList()/.AsEnumerable()/.AsAsyncEnumerable()` before L190, no group-aggregate over an IGrouping, and it is not a standalone top-level local query. The `validity` projection at L187 must therefore be translated to SQL.

(b) WOULD 500 â€” confirmed. `DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays` (Helpers/SendMailShim.cs:68-71) is a plain user-defined static CLR method (`public static DateTime? AddDays(DateTime? date, int? days) => date?.AddDays(days ?? 0);`), NOT EF.Functions.* and NOT a registered DbFunction. EF Core 10 has no SQL mapping for it. Overload resolution: `b.QuotDate` is non-nullable `DateTime` (Models/Quotation.cs:13) and the days arg `(b.QuotValidity==null)?0:b.QuotValidity+1` is `int?` (QuotValidity is `int?`, Models/Quotation.cs:43), so it binds to `AddDays(DateTime?, int?)` returning `DateTime?`. Because this untranslatable CLR call sits inside the SELECT projection, EF Core 10 throws InvalidOperationException ("The LINQ expression ... could not be translated") at translation time â€” BEFORE any rows are read â€” so it 500s on EVERY call regardless of data (even zero rows). No ConfigureWarnings/EnableSensitiveDataLogging/translation-error-downgrade config exists anywhere in the tree to soften this.

PORT REGRESSION vs legacy â€” confirmed. Legacy _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/CustomerRelationController.cs:204 used `DbFunctions.AddDays(b.QuotDate, (b.QuotValidity==null)?0:b.QuotValidity+1)`. EF6's System.Data.Entity.DbFunctions.AddDays IS a canonical function that translates to SQL DATEADD, so the identical query worked in EF6. The port mechanically swapped DbFunctions â†’ DbFunctionsCompat (a non-translatable CLR shim), which is exactly the break. Same pattern exists in HomeController.cs:229, QuotationController.cs:3520, PurchaseQuotationController.cs:1034, MyReportsController.cs:372/495, Common.cs:9931/10817 (same class of bug, separate findings).

Severity: HIGH â€” 500s on every request to GetQuotEntry, no data needed.

NOTE on the hunter's proposed fix: it is WRONG/won't compile. The hunter proposed `b.QuotDate.Value.AddDays(... (b.QuotValidity+1).Value)`. But `QuotDate` is non-nullable `DateTime`, so `.Value` does not exist â†’ compile error. The correct fix calls `.AddDays` directly on `b.QuotDate`. (The claim itself is still correct that it's a server-side EF Core 10 port-break that 500s; only the suggested patch text is defective.)
